task_completion.description: Create a clinical evaluation for patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GNU Health window showing a clinical evaluation, encounter, or patient record specifically for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Record an elevated temperature in the clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A temperature field in the clinical evaluation form showing a value of 38.5 or higher.

task_completion.description: Document an exposure diagnosis from the ICD-10 J68 or T59 family.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagnosis section or tab showing an ICD-10 code starting with 'J68' or 'T59' associated with the patient's evaluation.
A prescription or medication order section showing 'Ibuprofen', 'Paracetamol', or 'Acetaminophen'.
A lab orders or diagnostic tests section showing at least two distinct tests ordered for the patient.
An appointment or follow-up scheduling screen showing an appointment set for 1 to 3 days after the current system date.

integrity.description: Agent used the GNU Health GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GNU Health desktop client interface rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Data was entered into the actual application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Information is typed into the correct fields within GNU Health, not just written in a text editor or notes app.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing psql or sqlite3 commands modifying the GNU Health database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is John Zenon. Diagnosis must be from the J68 or T59 ICD-10 families. Clinical evaluation must record a temperature >= 38.5. Prescription must be for Ibuprofen, Paracetamol, or Acetaminophen. At least 2 diagnostic tests must be ordered. Follow-up appointment must be scheduled within 1 to 3 days.
